channel.title: SevenAtSevenLastSection
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenLastSection, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Irish Champ Potatoes
item.description: Irish champ potatoes are mashed potatoes done right. Fluffy spuds meet warm onion kissed milk and a generous melt of…

item.title: Book Riot’s Deals of the Day for February 18, 2026
item.description: An unconventional family's dark secrets, a bookshop sanctuary, a grumpy/sunshine rom-dram set in the 1950s, and more of today's book deals.

item.title: Why Graphic Nonfiction Matters
item.description: If you’re looking for a place to start with graphic nonfiction, here are eight titles that span across a range of topics and art styles.

item.title: Books for Avid Toni Morrison Fans
item.description: In the last few years, a host of writers and scholars have been putting out stunning volumes about Morrison’s work—here are a few of our favorites.

item.title: Sylvia Plath, Godly Chaos, and More New YA Book Releases for February 18, 2026
item.description: Chaotic gods, a story inspired by the real Sylvia Plath, love on ice, and more of this week's new YA book releases.

item.title: Book Recommendations for Fans of His & Hers
item.description: Plus, murder mysteries with family drama, Good Morning America picks a thriller for their February book club selection, and more.

item.title: How Often Does Child Welfare Call Police in Your State for Positive Drug Tests at Birth?
item.description: The Marshall Project found 70,000+ allegations of pregnancy drug use referred to law enforcement, often based on unreliable drug tests.

item.description: The agency wants to warehouse immigration detainees. Local residents and officials are raising concerns.

item.description: Hospitals routinely use unreliable drug tests at childbirth, and many parents are reported to child welfare agencies and the police. Here’s what you c...

item.title: Iowa’s Top Court Ended Court Fees in Dismissed Cases. Politicians Want Them Back
item.description: The state can no longer saddle defendants with court fees when charges are dismissed. Prosecutors are backing legislation to restore them.
